title: Men's March Madness 2026 bracket: NCAA tournament men's teams
content: The men's bracket has been revealed. Here's what you need to know about every competitor for the national title.

title: Team USA stifles D.R.'s offense to make WBC final
content: The United States held the Dominican Republic's vaunted offense in check to advance to the World Baseball Classic final with a 2-1 victory.

title: D.R. frustrated by blown call, but 'part of the game'
content: Two hours after a blown ball-strike call ended the Dominican Republic's World Baseball Classic, players emerged from the team's clubhouse with smiles, music and a message.

title: Duke top men's seed as Miami (OH) sneaks in
content: Duke, Arizona, Michigan and Florida were named the top seeds in the men's NCAA tournament with one-loss Miami (Ohio) grabbing an at-large bid.

title: UConn overall 1-seed; joined by UCLA, Texas, SC
content: Undefeated UConn was awarded the top overall seed in the women's NCAA tournament Sunday. The Huskies are joined by UCLA, Texas and South Carolina as the other No. 1 seeds.

title: Young birdies 17, wins Players on Fitzpatrick miss
content: Cameron Young made a tying 9½-foot putt for birdie on 17 and then won the Players Championship on 18 after Matt Fitzpatrick missed an 8-foot par putt.

title: Sources: Goedert stays with Eagles on new deal
content: Tight end Dallas Goedert reached agreement Sunday on a one-year deal to return to the Eagles for his ninth season in Philadelphia, sources told ESPN's Adam Schefter.

title: NBA: Missed call late in Lakers-Nuggets thriller
content: The NBA ruled that Spencer Jones had a clean strip of Austin Reaves with 9.2 seconds remaining in regulation of Saturday's Nuggets-Lakers game and that Reaves should not have gone to the free throw line.

title: Sinner wins Indian Wells, shouts out F1's Antonelli
content: Jannik Sinner beat Daniil Medvedev in the Indian Wells final 7-6 (6), 7-6 (4) on Sunday, winning seven straight points to rally from a 4-0 deficit in the second-set tiebreak and claim the title without dropping a single set.

title: ✍️ Vols track lands Kevin Hart's son, Hendrix
content: Hendrix Hart's personal bests include 10.68 seconds in the 100-meter dash and 21.35 in the 200-meter dash.

title: Takeaways from Team USA's thrilling WBC semifinal win over Dominican Republic
content: A star-studded World Baseball Classic showdown lived up to the hype. Here's how the U.S. moved on to the final.

title: How March Madness foes can end UConn's run through bracket
content: The Huskies are six wins away from a 13th title. Coaches and analysts gave us a blueprint for how to beat them.

title: As fans turn on their club, time is running out for Liverpool to save their season
content: Boos greeted Liverpool at the end of a 1-1 draw to Tottenham at Anfield that signaled further decline for the reigning Premier League champions.

title: NHL playoff watch: Projecting the Ducks' Pacific title chances
content: Here's how Anaheim can pull it off. Plus, full playoff projections, lottery standings after a busy Saturday.

title: Which NFL teams have improved the most in free agency? Five rosters that made the best moves
content: We picked out the teams that took the biggest steps forward this week through their signings and other moves -- including the 49ers.

title: Live tracking NFL free agency: Updates on signings, trades, cuts
content: We're following NFL free agency signings, trades, cuts, contract restructures and other moves as they happen.

title: Biggest questions facing Bears after free agent exodus
content: Chicago needs to figure out things at edge and in the secondary after a number of players left in free agency.

title: Steelers' offseason moves hint at Mike McCarthy's offensive plan
content: Pittsburgh's additions of Michael Pittman Jr. and Rico Dowdle portend a "physical, run-first offense" in 2026.

title: March Madness: Odds, spreads and totals for every men's opening round games
content: Moneylines, spreads, totals and BPI predictions for the first round of the 2026 NCAA men's tournament.

title: Chinese Grand Prix: Antonelli wins in Shanghai, Mercedes go 1-2 again - as it happened
content: Kimi Antonelli converted his record-breaking Chinese Grand Prix pole into a maiden Formula 1 victory on Sunday, you can relive exactly how it happened here.

title: WNBA CBA negotiations: New deal by Monday is goal as marathon meeting continues
content: The WNBA and union exchanged about 15 proposals between Tuesday and the early hours of Saturday morning.

title: 'Banana Brees': Former Saints QB Drew Brees and Tyler Shough take in Savannah Bananas
content: Brees and Shough made appearances during the Savannah Bananas exhibition games at the Caesars Superdome, the home of the Saints.

title: Transfer rumors, news: Real Madrid plot move for Bayern's Olise
content: Real Madrid are plotting a deal for Bayern star Michael Olise, while Chelsea want to sign Messi... just not the Inter Miami one. Transfer Talk has the latest.

title: Cheat sheet: Sleepers, busts, rookies and closers
content: You've read their names all preseason long, it'd be a shame to forget them on draft day. The ESPN Cheat Sheet is one way to make sure that doesn't happen.

title: Watch: Fire erupts again at Fujairah port in UAE, harbour suspends oil loadings
content: Fujairah port in the UAE experienced a drone attack, igniting a fire at a petroleum facility. While emergency teams quickly contained the blaze with no reported injuries, oil loadings were suspended. This marks a recent escalation in attacks on the vital oil hub, underscoring regional tensions and the port's critical role in global energy trade.

title: Did PM Modi know about US-Israel's Iran strike plan? What Israeli envoy said
content: Israel's envoy, Reuven Azar, clarified that the military operation against Iran was timed post-PM Modi's visit to seize an "operational opportunity." He emphasized Israel's anticipation of Iranian retaliation and its commitment to neutralizing nuclear and missile threats, while also debunking disinformation about PM Netanyahu. Israel aims to prevent the Iranian regime from reconstituting its plans.

title: India-US trade deal: Commerce secy says ‘actual signing when new architecture in place’
content: The comments assume significance at a time when the US Supreme Court has struck down the tariffs that were imposed by the Trump administration, calling them illegal.

title: ‘You can make two-three XIs’: Surya says India’s T20 talent pool now ‘unlimited’
content: India's T20 talent pool is so deep, Suryakumar Yadav believes multiple international-quality teams could be formed, thanks to robust domestic cricket and the IPL.  He highlighted the current generation as India's strongest T20 group.  Under his captaincy, Team India boasts an impressive win rate, a testament to the team's collective mindset and his strong rapport with coach Gautam Gambhir.

title: Nvidia CEO 'realises' that Google and Meta are set to eat the company's lunch
content: Nvidia's dominance built on a single chip for all AI tasks is facing a challenge.  As inference, the running of AI models, becomes more crucial and cost-sensitive, customers are exploring cheaper, purpose-built alternatives from rivals like Google and Meta.  Jensen Huang's expected unveiling of a new inference-focused chip signals a potential shift away from Nvidia's long-held strategy.

title: Stock market today: Nifty50 surges past 23,300; BSE Sensex gains 800 points
content: Stock market today: Nifty50 and BSE Sensex rallied strongly in trade on Monday after two weeks of brutal selloff. While Nifty50 crossed 23,500, BSE Sensex rose over 1,000 points. A

title: Russia-Ukraine clashes: Explosions rock Kyiv in rare daytime attack
content: Kyiv faced a rare daytime Russian assault Monday as air defenses intercepted projectiles, with debris falling in the city center. This escalation follows Moscow's claim of repelling a major Ukrainian drone attack over the weekend. Meanwhile, US peace efforts are reportedly stalled amid the widening Middle East conflict, highlighting the growing role of drones in modern warfare.

title: Sachin Tendulkar’s 100th international century: The milestone that stunned the cricket world
content: Cricket icon Sachin Tendulkar achieved his historic 100th international century on March 16, 2012, against Bangladesh in the Asia Cup. This monumental feat, after a year-long wait, cemented his status as a legend. Despite the immense pressure, Tendulkar's patient 114-run innings showcased his enduring class, inspiring a generation.

title: Perplexity AI CEO agrees that Computer Science is gradually returning to its roots
content: AI is rapidly automating coding's routine tasks, pushing software engineering back towards its mathematical roots. Experts predict AI could soon handle most end-to-end coding, with some engineers already code-free. While LLMs still struggle with complex design, this shift is prompting a rethink in computer science education, emphasizing logic over syntax.

title: 1 dead, another injured in J&K landslide; 230 evacuated amid heavy snowfall
content: A landslide at a Kishtwar hydropower project claimed one life and injured another worker. Meanwhile, over 230 people, including women and children, were rescued after being stranded by heavy snowfall at Sinthan Top. Educational institutions outside municipal limits in Kishtwar and Doda were closed as a precaution.

title: Mechanic’s advice turns fatal: Man, 3 grandchildren die after inhaling toxic fumes from idling bike
content: A devastating incident in Annamayya district saw an elderly man and his three grandchildren perish in their sleep due to carbon monoxide poisoning. An idling motorcycle, kept running inside their home on a mechanic's advice, filled the house with deadly fumes overnight. The children's parents, sleeping elsewhere, discovered the tragedy upon waking.

title: FASTag annual pass gets pricier: Here's what changes from April 1
content: The National Highways Authority of India is increasing the FASTag Annual Pass fee. Starting April 1, 2026, the pass will cost Rs 3,075. This prepaid toll plan is for non-commercial private vehicles. The revision aligns with National Highways Fee Rules. Over 56 lakh users have adopted the pass. The new rate applies to eligible vehicles at approximately 1,150 fee plazas.

title: Red Sea alternative to Strait of Hormuz? Saudi's offer to oil buyers amid supply crunch
content: Saudi Arabia is offering long-term oil buyers a new route via its Red Sea port of Yanbu, bypassing the disrupted Strait of Hormuz. This move aims to ensure supply continuity for April shipments, though pipeline capacity limits full allocations. Asian buyers, including China's Sinopec, are already adjusting refinery operations due to supply concerns, highlighting global uncertainty over the conflict's duration.

title: Oscars 2026 complete winners list: Paul Thomas Anderson, Michael B Jordan win big
content: At the star-studded 98th Academy Awards, a plethora of cinematic triumphs were on display, with 'One Battle After Another' stealing the spotlight by winning Best Picture alongside numerous other awards. Jessie Buckley dazzled the audience and judges alike, scoring Best Actress for her moving role in 'Hamnet', while Michael B.

title: ‘Mama’ in the spotlight: Himanta Sarma emerges as Assam's most loved and loathed leader
content: Assam's Chief Minister Himanta Biswa Sarma, a former Congress rebel, is a polarizing figure, lauded for rebranding the state as a growth hub and combating infiltrators, while criticized for his combative rhetoric. Known affectionately as 'mama' by youth, Sarma's political acumen and social media presence are central to his strategy, even as he faces accusations of hate speech.

title: Trade deficit for Feb widens to $3.96 bn: India’s exports climb 11%, imports surge 21%
content: India's exports reached $76.13 billion in February 2026, showing strong growth. Imports rose even faster, leading to a wider trade deficit. For the fiscal year, exports are on track for growth. The country previously set a record in exports for the 2024-25 fiscal year. Challenges like the West Asia crisis may impact March exports.

title: The balancing act: How India is stepping up engagement with Iran as Hormuz crisis deepens
content: India is prioritizing diplomatic engagement with Iran to ensure safe passage through the Strait of Hormuz, offering a stark contrast to the US military approach. New Delhi's direct talks have already yielded results, with Indian tankers resuming transit. This strategy aims to secure India's crucial energy imports while carefully managing its relationship with the United States.

title: Major reshuffle in Bengal: EC removes DGP, Kolkata CP and other top bureaucrats ahead of polls
content: The Election Commission ordered the transfer of top bureaucrats in West Bengal, including the Chief Secretary and Principal Secretary of Home and Hill Affairs, citing preparedness for upcoming assembly elections. This move led to a walkout by TMC MPs from the Rajya Sabha, who accused the EC of misusing its powers against the elected government.

title: Haryana: Husband arrested after woman and 3 daughters found dead in Nuh well
content: A husband has been arrested for abetting the suicide of his wife and three daughters found dead in a well. Allegations of severe domestic abuse, dowry harassment, and pressure for a male heir surfaced following the tragic incident. The wife's father claims she was pushed, while police investigate the husband's role and potential accomplices.

title: ‘I want to break that’: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi targets Chris Gayle’s iconic IPL record
content: Teen sensation Vaibhav Sooryavanshi aims to shatter Chris Gayle's iconic 175-run T20 record in the upcoming IPL season. After a stellar 2025, including a U-19 World Cup final century, the Rajasthan Royals star is focused on team victory while eyeing individual milestones. He credits the franchise and Rahul Dravid for his rapid development.

title: After Arijit Singh, Shreya Ghoshal says 'I also feel like taking a break sometimes'
content: After Arijit Singh’s decision to step away from playback singing sparked discussion among fans, Shreya Ghoshal has shared her thoughts on the singer’s move and the importance of authenticity in live performances. The singer stressed that lip-syncing on stage is something she would never want to rely on. She also described Arijit as someone deeply connected to music.

title: US-Iran war hits stock market: Citi, Nomura cut Nifty50 target prices; here’s the outlook
content: Citi has revised its Nifty target to 27,000 from the earlier estimate of 28,500. The new projection suggests a potential upside of about 17 percent from the index’s last closing level.

title: ‘Pakistan becoming an associate team’: Ex-cricketer slams Afridi, team management
content: Ahmed Shehzad, a former Pakistan cricketer, has strongly criticized the team's leadership following their ODI series defeat to Bangladesh. He expressed concerns about the declining standards in Pakistan cricket. Shehzad questioned the management's decisions and their handling of player availability. The team's recent performance has drawn significant criticism.

title: High speed alone can't establish rash driving: Why K'taka HC acquitted man in road accident case
content: The High Court concluded that both the trial court and the appellate court had erred in convicting the accused without sufficient evidence establishing rash and negligent driving.

title: Hardik Pandya drives Ferrari 12Cilindri In Mumbai: Why's this car special
content: Star cricketer Hardik Pandya was recently spotted driving Ferrari's newest front-engined V12 grand tourer, the 12Cilindri, in Mumbai. This stunning supercar, inspired by the legendary 365 GTB/4 Daytona, boasts a powerful 6.5-litre V12 engine producing 830 hp and can accelerate from 0 to 100 kmph in just 2.9 seconds. The 12Cilindri, priced around Rs 8.

title: Rasgulla stuck in throat: Man chokes to death at wedding feast
content: A tragic incident unfolded at a wedding feast in Jamshedpur, Jharkhand, where a 41-year-old man, Lalit Singh, succumbed to death. A rasgulla reportedly became lodged in his throat, completely blocking his airway and cutting off oxygen supply. He collapsed during the meal and tragically passed away within minutes despite immediate efforts to seek medical assistance.

title: Teen driving at 118 kmph crashes into farmhouse wall in Gujarat, 2 dead
content: A 20-year-old man, recently released from jail, died after his speeding car crashed into a farmhouse wall in Rajkot. He had reportedly posted an Instagram story showing the car's speed at 118 kmph shortly before the fatal accident. Another occupant, a 16-year-old, also succumbed to injuries, while a third person remains hospitalized.

title: Are Trump and Netanyahu on same page? What US President said amidst rumors of rift
content: President Trump denied reports of a rift with Israeli Prime Minister Netanyahu, calling them 'fake news' and describing their relationship as 'extraordinary.' This comes amid ongoing US-Israeli military operations against Iran, which have destabilized the region and impacted global markets. While sharing short-term military goals, their long-term objectives for Iran appear to differ.

title: Watch: Nor’wester, tornado hit Odisha’s Mayurbhanj; several homes damaged, 2 dead, 29 injured
content: A severe nor'wester and tornado struck four villages in Mayurbhanj district, claiming two lives and injuring 29 others. The storm caused extensive damage to houses, uprooted trees, and blew away vehicles and people. The Chief Minister announced ex-gratia for the deceased and free treatment for the injured.

title: Gold price today: How much 18K, 22K and 24K gold cost in your city; check rates
content: Gold futures experienced a significant drop of Rs 2,225 to Rs 1.56 lakh per 10 grams, influenced by a stronger US dollar and weaker international markets. Analysts attribute the decline to rising energy prices and diminished expectations for Federal Reserve interest rate cuts, impacting investor sentiment and leading to outflows from gold-backed ETFs.

title: ‘Will you steal ICC trophies now?’ Ex-cricketer blasts Pakistan after loss to Bangladesh
content: Pakistan's cricket woes deepened with a 2-1 ODI series loss to Bangladesh, drawing sharp criticism from former wicketkeeper Kamran Akmal. Akmal questioned the team's performance and administration, highlighting their struggles after an early T20 World Cup exit. Bangladesh secured the series win in Dhaka after a strong batting performance and effective bowling.

title: Broom, wiper, belt used to torture 4-year-old? UP man, 2nd wife held after boy dies
content: Chowk police in Lucknow recovered household objects, including a broom, knife, and belt, suspected of being used to torture a four-year-old boy. The child died with 18 injury marks and multiple fractures, indicating sustained abuse. His father and stepmother have been arrested and are being questioned.

title: ‘Reason & coordinate’: Jaishankar reveals how India secured two tankers’ passage through Hormuz
content: External affairs minister S Jaishankar hailed direct talks with Iran as the most effective way to resume shipping through the Strait of Hormuz. Tehran has allowed Indian-flagged vessels to pass, demonstrating the success of diplomacy over potential military action. This approach offers a potential model for other nations grappling with disrupted energy supplies.

title: US forced labour probe: China urges Trump to 'correct erroneous' trade practices
content: China has lodged formal objections with the US over new trade investigations into forced labor and excess industrial capacity, urging Washington to correct its "erroneous ways." The probes, targeting 60 economies including China, come as trade negotiators meet in Paris. Beijing criticizes the investigations as "political manipulation" and "discriminatory," while the US cites concerns over forced labor in Xinjiang.

title: Iran’s Sejjil-2 'dancing missile': The solid-fuel weapon that can strike 2,000 km away
content: Iran's IRGC launched a significant missile barrage, including the advanced Sejjil, targeting Israeli military sites in retaliation for recent strikes. This marks the first use of Sejjil missiles since the conflict escalated on February 28. The operation, dubbed 'True Promise 4', involved multiple ballistic missile types, aiming at strategic locations and defense industries.

title: Eid Ul Fitr Moon sighting 2026: When will India, Pak, Saudi Arabia, UAE and Iran celebrate?
content: The final days of Ramadan bring a familiar anticipation for the moon sighting, determining the start of Eid al-Fitr. In 2026, this could be March 20 or 21, with celebrations often split globally. Beyond the festive meals and new outfits, Eid emphasizes community, charity, and gratitude for completing the month of fasting.

title: Why are gold prices not rising too much amid US-Iran war? Key levels to watch for
content: Gold price prediction today: Gold prices are supported by the underlying geopolitical negative sentiment but the gains are capped, says Manav Modi, Senior Analyst, Commodity Research at Motilal Oswal Financial Services Ltd.

title: ‘I’ll hit it’: Samson reveals Sooryavanshi’s fearless chat with Dravid before IPL debut
content: Rajasthan Royals' young sensation Vaibhav Sooryavanshi surprised coach Rahul Dravid with his fearless approach before his IPL debut.  Samson revealed Sooryavanshi's confident response to Dravid's queries, stating he'd 'just play' and 'hit the first ball in the air' if in his zone.  Sooryavanshi, the youngest IPL debutant, aims to help RR win the trophy in IPL 2026.

title: Iran war impact: Oil industry leaders warn Trump admin fuel crunch may worsen
content: Top oil executives warned the Trump administration that the energy crisis stemming from the Iran conflict could worsen.  Concerns were raised about disruptions to shipments through the Strait of Hormuz, a critical oil transit route.  Despite President Trump's prediction of falling oil prices once the conflict subsides, his call for allies to deploy warships has met with negative responses.

title: Siblings die in motorcycle accident in Kilimanoor in Kerala capital
content: Their motorcycle reportedly rammed an electric post and skidded on the road before falling into a nearby drain

title: Look into complaint about wrong classification of Mutharaiyar community: Madras HC to T.N. govt.
content: Justice P.T. Asha directs the Backward Classes, Most Backward Classes, and Denotified Communities secretary to dispose of a representation made in this regard within 12 weeks

title: Kerala Assembly elections: CPI rebel MLA C.C. Mukundan joins BJP, eyes Nattika contest
content: Mukundan says decision part of his “fight” after Congress entry bid fails; expresses interest in contesting on BJP symbol in Nattika

title: LPG tanker overturns near Katapady, Mangaluru-Udupi traffic diverted amid gas leak
content: HPCL officials have been called to the spot to arrest the leakage and move aside the tanker. It is likely to take about four hours to complete the work.

title: Munambam land dispute: Kerala Waqf Board chairperson says ‘humanitarian approach’ needed in residents’ case
content: K.S. Hamsa, who stuck to the board’s earlier position that the controversial holding at Munambam was indeed a Waqf property, blames Farook College, the Mutawalli (the manager of the Waqf), for the land dispute

title: Kharge raises LPG shortage issue in Rajya Sabha; government says Opposition trying to bring anarchy
content: Mr. Kharge said that the energy supply crisis has exposed the government’s pathetic management and flawed foreign policy

title: Why nurses in Kerala’s private hospitals went on strike | Explained
content: Nurses at private hospitals across Kerala went on an ‘indefinite strike’ from March 9 to 13, led by United Nurses Association, demanding higher wages and better working conditions. Though the strike, which disrupted critical services, has been temporarily withdrawn after a Kerala High Court directive, UNA says it will relaunch the strike if there is no progress in mediation efforts

title: Supreme Court refuses to intervene in a plea filed by journalist Ravi Nair against Ahmedabad Crime Branch notice
content: The court allowed the petitioner to withdraw the petition without entertaining his oral plea for protection against any coercive action for now

title: Supreme Court quashes criminal proceedings against Ashoka University professor after Haryana refuses prosecution sanction
content: The Haryana Police had arrested professor Mahmudabad on May 18. It was alleged that his social media posts on Operation Sindoor had endangered the sovereignty and integrity of the country

title: Two arrested for murder of AR constable in Chilkanagar
content: The deceased was identified as Paruvula Sudheer Kumar, 32, an AR constable and resident of RTC Colony in Chengicherla

title: Arunachal government transfers 27 senior police officers in major reshuffle
content: According to a notification, the reshuffle has been done to streamline police administration and bolster the law and order machinery in the northeastern State

title: Supreme Court asks NGO to raise concerns about Bhopal gas tragedy residual ash before HC
content: The victims’ rights NGO said a 2015 CPCB report showed 337 metric tons of toxic waste having high mercury concentrations. However, 2025 report “inexplicably” detects no mercury in the residual ash of the incinerated waste

title: 'Illegal' call centre case: Enforcement Directorate conducts raids in West Bengal
content: It was not immediately clear if the probe is linked to some political funding or inducements linked to the electoral process

title: Kerala Assembly polls: Poster war erupts in UDF in Kasaragod ahead of candidate announcement
content: Posters, reportedly put up in the name of IUML workers, urge the party not to field him and accuse him of being an “imported” candidate

title: Kerala Assembly polls: Congress throws weight behind former CPI(M) leader G. Sudhakaran in Ambalappuzha
content: Congress screening committee has decided not to field a party candidate in Ambalappuzha and to extend support to dissident CPI(M) leader G. Sudhakaran instead, according to sources

title: Plugging in: Why India must electrify its kitchens at scale
content: An IEEFA study from October 2025 found that electric cooking is 37% cheaper than non-subsidised LPG and 14% cheaper than piped natural gas for a family of four in Delhi — without any electricity subsidy

title: Election Commission removes Chief Secretary, Home Secretary in poll-bound West Bengal
content: Kolkata Police Commissioner, DGP West Bengal, and ADG Law and Order have also been transferred; ECI said its directions are to be implemented with immediate effect and asked for a report of the joining of the two officers in their respective offices by 3 p.m. on March 16

title: Light to moderate rain likely in Hyderabad on March 16, says IMD
content: “City is expected to experience a partly cloudy sky through the day. “Light to moderate rain or thundershowers accompanied by gusty winds are likely to occur in parts of the city towards the evening or night,” says IMD

title: Watch: The ruling party and the opposition must rise above partisanship | The Hindu Editorial
content: The Lok Sabha rejected by voice vote, on March 11, a resolution under Article 94(C) of the Constitution for the removal of Lok Sabha Speaker Om Birla from office, but only after a hostile debate on the functioning of the House deepened the wedge between the government and the Opposition.

title: No Eid celebrations for Shias in MP in the wake of Khamenei's death in the Iran-Iraq War
content: "Around 4,000 Shia families are living in Madhya Pradesh. We will not wear new clothes or celebrate Eid this year as we are saddened by Khamenei's death. We will only offer namaz," says All India Shia Samaj State president

title: History-sheeter shot dead by police near Chennai’s Madhavaram
content: He is said to have been involved in at least 30 criminal cases, including five murders and a dacoity case reported in Puzhal recently

title: West Asia conflict: Emirates flight from Kerala’s Thiruvananthapuram returns due to Dubai airport closure
content: The flight, carrying 353 passengers and 19 crew members, returned midway. Passengers will be shifted to the terminal, according to airport authorities in the Kerala capital

title: West Asia conflict: Dubai airport closure forces Emirates flight back to Kochi
content: Emirates flight with 325 passengers returned to Kochi, Kerala, following a ‘security incident’. Flight EK 533, which departed at 4:30 a.m., landed back in India at 8:30 a.m

title: It’s ironic welfare schemes implemented in T.N. are portrayed as fiscally irresponsible: Thangam Thennarasu
content: Our aim has always been an inclusive growth. Economists and development experts have recognised that direct transfer of benefits reduces leakages, provides autonomy to beneficiaries in deciding how best to utilise the support, and stimulates local and rural economies, he says

title: Why is Rajasthan’s property Bill drawing scrutiny? | Explained
content: How will the proposed law regulate property transfers in notified areas? What concerns has it triggered?

title: 10 patients killed, several others injured in major fire at Cuttack’s SCB Medical College Hospital
content: Chief Minister Mohan Charan Majhi, who visited the hospital, ordered a judicial probe into the incident

title: Kerala Assembly polls 2026: With five wins on the trot, Congress pins hopes on Paravur, CPI hopeful of wresting seat
content: The CPI, which won the seat in the maiden contest in 1957, emerged victorious the last time in 1996. Since then, it has been V.D. Satheesan all the way. This time, the Left party is fielding E.T. Taison against him

title: U.K. military bases given to U.S. to reduce impact of war on millions of Indians in Gulf, says U.K. Deputy High Commissioner
content: U.K. Foreign Secretary has been in touch to express the country’s solidarity with partners in the region, including the UAE, Saudi Arabia, Bahrain, Oman, Qatar, and Kuwait — many of whom have been targeted with Iranian strikes, which we strongly condemn, says Shalini Medepalli

title: Air India, AI Express to operate 48 flights connecting West Asia from today
content: The two airlines will have their scheduled operations to Jeddah and Muscat, including a total of 10 flights between Indian cities and Jeddah, a release said

title: DNT leaders move Supreme Court seeking distinct enumeration in 2027 Census
content: Petition seeks specific question on Denotified, Nomadic and Semi-Nomadic Tribes in houselisting and population enumeration phases of the 2027 Census

title: Over 30 vehicles stranded amid snowfall in J&K's Kishtwar; rescue operation under way
content: The vehicles got stuck due to fresh snowfall and slippery road conditions at the high-altitude passes, disrupting traffic movement on the road connecting Kishtwar in Jammu with south Kashmir's Anantnag district

title: U.S. attitude to the Kurdish question reflects colonial mindset, says Nilufer Koc
content: Spokesperson of Kurdistan National Congress says the U.S. made the proposal about Kurdish fighters without broader consultation with the Kurds

title: SIR: Tamil Nadu’s urban areas record higher reduction in number of voters than rural ones
content: Looking at the overall picture, urban constituencies in northern and western districts have experienced a perceptible decrease, whereas those in the central and southern regions have seen a moderate decline

title: DMK steps up seat-sharing discussions with allies
content: The talks between the DMK and the Left parties will resume on March 17; VCK leaders say at least 10 seats will justify the party’s strength and convince the cadre

title: T.N. Assembly election 2026: campaign window set at 38 days, same as in 2021
content: This period is crucial for parties contesting in alliances, as they will have to finalise seat-sharing numbers, identify constituencies, select candidates, file nominations, release manifestos, and draw up campaign itineraries

title: New FOB across railway track in Tiruvannamalai to be ready in April for public use
content: Officials of State Highways say that the new bridge will help commuters reach the railway station safely because at present, they have to cross the busy Chittoor - Cuddalore High Road

title: LPG bookings fall to 77 lakh; Indian crude tanker sails out of UAE’s Fujairah safely
content: All domestic ‘refineries are operating at high capacity and maintaining adequate crude oil inventories,’ the government says as Jag Laadki becomes the fourth Indian-flagged vessel to have come out of the war zone unharmed

title: Pak terrorist killed near LoC in Baramulla, says Army
content: The Army says “warlike stores” including an AK rifle, pistols and large amounts of ammunition have been recovered and the operation continues

title: Seven pilgrims from Tamil Nadu  killed, seven others injured in road accident in Nepal
content: A total of 24 pilgrims from Pollachi and Devakottai travelled to the Manakamana Temple in Gorkha district. The bodies are being taken to Kathmandu, from where they will be sent to Chennai, says a State government statement

title: Lands or homes forcibly seized from terror-affected families will be liberated, returned: J&K L-G Manoj Sinha
content: He hands over appointment letters to 27 kin, says pending cases will be reopened to hold perpetrators accountable

title: Parties welcome two-phase West Bengal polls; people will bring down TMC, says BJP
content: In 2021, the Assembly election in West Bengal was held in eight phases; in 2011 and 2016 there were six phases; the announcement for this year’s poll is a departure from the past

title: Ahead of Id, NC seeks release of Kashmiris booked under UAPA for protesting Khamenei killing
content: National Conference describes protest rallies as “mourning gatherings”, noting that Khamenei was not just a head of state, but a revered religious leader to many Kashmiris; MLAs say L-G promised to review cases ahead of Id

title: Hours before Rajya Sabha polling, AIMIM announces to vote for RJD candidate
content: NDA which is falling short of the 3 votes claiming to win all five seat whereas main opposition party RJD relying on five MLA of (AIMIM) and lone MLA of BSP.

title: Government response to West Asia conflict driven by supreme national interest: RSS

title: Parties, outfits invoke ‘Brahmin’ angle to ‘pandit’ reference in U.P. police recruitment exam
content: A question asked candidates to choose a term for ‘a person who changes according to circumstances’. The options were ‘Pandit’ (scholar), ‘Avsarvadi’ (opportunist), ‘Nishkapat’ (innocent or sincere) and ‘Sadachari’ (virtuous). CM issues directions to avoid ‘derogatory’ and ‘disrespectful’ references

title: Diversify agriculture and encourage farmers to grow pulses, Supreme Court tells government
content: Besides a relook into policy and diversification of crops, the Supreme Court of India asks Centre to convene a meeting of stakeholders to look into various issues, including the absence of MSP for pulses

title: UPSC’s new rule: Supreme Court nod must for delay in choosing State DGPs
content: Attorney General tells Supreme Court that there is no “provision empowering the UPSC to condone such inordinate delay”; States cannot appoint acting DGP as there is no such concept, says top court

title: CBI arrests ‘recruiter’ linked to overseas cyber scam centres exploiting Indian nationals
content: The accused allegedly collected about $300-400 per head from jobseekers and facilitated their travel to Cambodia, where they were forced to carry out online frauds targeting victims in India

title: Over 900 chicken pox cases reported in Ernakulam in 2026
content: Across the State, 1,897 cases were reported this month alone, taking the total this year to 11,388, according to the Health department data

title: Hospitals canteens in Kochi struggling amid LPG crisis

title: TDP issues show-cause notice to Eluru MP over drugs allegations
content: The party directs Putta Mahesh Kumar to submit explanation within five days and advises him to stay away from party activities pending review

title: PM lauds Speaker for conducting Lok Sabha proceedings with ‘dedication, patience and impartiality’
content: PM congratulates MPs for rejecting “political misconduct” by defeating the Opposition’s motion to remove the Speaker; says it’s a matter of concern when political disagreement turns into disrespect for parliamentary dignity

title: Bypolls in Davangere South and Bagalkot could witness intense battle

title: LDF declares 10 candidates for Kozhikode, UDF, NDA yet to announce nominees

title: Assam inks settlement with Hmar, Kuki extremist groups

title: LDF steals a march on UDF by naming candidates within an hour of Kerala poll date announcement

title: Civic polls will be held in May, DKS tells ticket aspirants

title: IndiGo, Adani Airport Launch Partnership For BluChips On Duty-Free Shopping

title: Class 10 Dropout From Bengal Caught Running Fake Clinic in Gujarat
content: During a surprise inspection, officials caught Sanjit Nilkomal Biswas actively examining patients at an "Asha Clinic" in a Surat village.

title: 20 Thai Sailors Return Home After Vessel Attacked In Gulf, 3 Still Stranded
